items.title: USDA approves SNAP food-choice waivers; several states begin restrictions in 2026
items.summary: USDA announced it signed state waivers that change what SNAP benefits can buy in Arkansas, Idaho, and Utah (along with earlier waivers for Indiana, Iowa, and Nebraska). The USDA release says Idaho and Utah waivers take effect Jan. 1, 2026, and Arkansas’ waiver takes effect July 1, 2026, excluding items like soda/soft drinks and candy (details vary by state).

items.title: CMS issues guidance on limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1
items.summary: CMS issued guidance (summarized by AHA) explaining that beginning Oct. 1, federal matching funds for full Medicaid and CHIP benefits are restricted to legal permanent residents and a limited set of other groups; other lawfully present noncitizens (including refugees and asylees) are no longer eligible for full-benefit federal matching funds, though emergency Medicaid matching funds remain available. The guidance says states must conduct eligibility redeterminations, update systems, and submit Medicaid/CHIP state plan amendments to implement the change.

items.title: HUD changes documentation rules for Emergency Housing Vouchers and Stability Vouchers (SSN and eligible immigration status)
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-05 removes a waiver that allowed public housing agencies to admit Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) applicants without first obtaining and verifying Social Security number documentation and evidence of eligible noncitizen status. The notice requires agencies to follow existing verification rules and says PHAs must comply within 60 days of the notice’s publication (with a 180-day window referenced for continued assistance eligibility, pending verification and any good-faith extensions).
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: USDA sets FY2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts; April 2026 deadline for added forms of produce
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants, reflecting an 11.8% inflation adjustment over the FY 2022 baseline. The memo also notes that WIC state agencies must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SSA posts 2026 SSI federal payment amounts (individual $994/month; couple $1,491/month)
items.summary: The Social Security Administration’s SSI COLA page lists the 2026 monthly maximum federal SSI amounts as $994 for an eligible individual and $1,491 for an eligible couple (with $498 for an essential person). SSA notes these amounts reflect the 2.8% COLA that becomes effective January 2026.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; higher payments start Jan. 2026 (SSI starts Dec. 31, 2025)
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026 affecting Social Security benefits and SSI payments for about 75 million Americans. SSA said nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries will see the COLA beginning in January 2026, while increased payments to nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients begin on Dec. 31, 2025, and the average retirement benefit increase is about $56 per month.

items.title: SSA 2026 COLA fact sheet: higher disability earnings thresholds and updated SSI figures for 2026
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet lists 2026 Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) at $1,690/month for non-blind disabled workers and $2,830/month for blind workers, and a Trial Work Period (TWP) amount of $1,210/month. The same fact sheet lists the 2026 federal SSI payment standard as $994/month for an individual and $1,491/month for a couple.

items.title: Federal Student Aid confirms maximum Pell Grant award is $7,395 for the 2026–27 award year
items.summary: StudentAid.gov states that the maximum Federal Pell Grant award for the 2026–27 award year is $7,395. The same article notes students must file the FAFSA to be considered and that some students may qualify for up to 150% of their yearly Pell award if attending an additional term (year-round Pell).

items.title: USDA posts SNAP income eligibility limits and maximum allotments for Oct. 2025–Sept. 2026
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP eligibility materials list income limits for applications submitted between Oct. 1, 2025 and Sept. 30, 2026 (for the 48 contiguous states and D.C.), including gross monthly income of $1,696 for a 1-person household and $2,292 for a 2-person household. USDA also lists maximum monthly allotments (48 states + D.C.) such as $298 for 1 person and $546 for 2 people.

items.title: USDA notes new limits on timing and method for future Thrifty Food Plan reevaluations (affecting SNAP benefit calculations)
items.summary: USDA’s Thrifty Food Plan page notes that federal law requires periodic reevaluation of the Thrifty Food Plan (which is used in SNAP benefit calculations). The page says the One Big Beautiful Bill Act requires the next reevaluation be no earlier than Oct. 1, 2027, and requires future reevaluations to be held cost-neutral.

items.title: Maryland announces Housing Choice Voucher waiting list opens April 1–30, 2026 (Eastern Shore)
items.summary: Maryland’s Department of Housing and Community Development announced its Housing Choice Voucher Program waiting list for the Eastern Shore region opens April 1, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. and closes April 30, 2026 at 5:00 p.m., with online-only applications. The notice highlights this as a time-limited application window for households seeking Section 8/HCV assistance in that region.
items.source: Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development
